confidence.def: 自信、信任、祕密、自信的人的人的人物 (單數)
confidence.col: in confidence, lack of confidence, vote of confidence
confidence.ex: She has a lot of confidence in her own ability.

confident.ex: He gave a very confident smile before the race.

confidential.def: 機密的、信任的人的人的人物 (密)
confidential.ex: This report contains strictly confidential data.

confidentiality.def: 機密性、祕密性的人物 (單數)
confidentiality.ex: Client confidentiality is vital for the law firm today.

confidentially.ex: They spoke confidentially about the new project.

confiding.def: 信任的、不懷疑的人的人 (或物)
confiding.ex: She has a very confiding and kind nature today.

confidingly.ex: She whispered confidingly in her friend's ear.

configurable.def: 可配置的人的人物 (單數)
configurable.ex: The software is highly configurable for users.

configuration.def: 配置、結構、輪廓的人物 (單數)
configuration.ex: The system configuration needs some adjustments.

configure.def: 配置、結構、設定的人的人
configure.ex: You need to configure the settings correctly now.

confine.def: 禁閉、限制、邊界的人的人人物 (單數)
confine.col: confine to, solitary confinement, within the confines of
confine.ex: Please don't confine the animal in a small cage.

confined.def: 受限的、狹窄的人的人 (或物)
confined.ex: Working in a confined space is very difficult tonight.

confinement.def: 禁閉、限制、分娩的人物 (單數)
confinement.ex: The prisoner was kept in solitary confinement today.

confirm.def: 確認、證實、批准、堅定的人的人
confirm.col: confirm the news, confirm a booking
confirm.ex: Please call later to confirm your hotel booking.

confirmation.def: 確認、證實、堅信的人物 (單數)
confirmation.ex: We are still waiting for official confirmation today.

confirmed.def: 確定的、成習慣的人的人 (或物)
confirmed.ex: He is a confirmed bachelor who loves his freedom.

confiscate.def: 沒收、充公、徵用的人的人
confiscate.ex: The teacher had to confiscate the student's phone.

confiscation.ex: Mass confiscation of property occurred in the war.

conflagration.def: 大火、武裝衝突人物 (單數)
conflagration.ex: The huge conflagration destroyed the whole factory.

conflict.def: 衝突、鬥爭、矛盾、抵觸的人的人的人物
conflict.col: conflict of interest, armed conflict, in conflict
conflict.ex: There was a sharp conflict of interest in local deal.

conflicting.ex: There were conflicting reports about the accident.

confluence.ex: The city was built at the confluence of rivers.

conform.def: 符合、遵照、順應的人的人
conform.ex: The product must conform to local safety rules.

conformance.ex: The design is in conformance with requirements.

conformation.def: 構造、形狀、一致性人物 (單數)
conformation.ex: The physical conformation of the horse was great.

conformist.def: 遵奉者、隨大流的人人物 (單數)
conformist.ex: He was a social conformist who followed all trends.

conformity.def: 符合、一致、遵從的人物 (單數)
conformity.ex: There was a lack of conformity in their reports.

confound.def: 使困惑、打敗、混淆的人的人
confound.ex: The results managed to confound all expectations.

confrere.def: 同行、同事、會友人物 (單數)
confrere.ex: He met with a distinguished confrere from US.

confront.def: 對面、對質、遭遇、比較的人的人
confront.ex: He finally decided to confront the terrible truth.

confrontation.def: 對質、衝突、對抗的人物 (單數)
confrontation.ex: They wanted to avoid a direct confrontation tonight.

confrontational.def: 對抗的人、挑釁的人的人物
confrontational.ex: His confrontational attitude caused a lot of pain.

confucian.def: 孔子的、儒教的人的人物 (單數)
confucian.ex: Confucian ethics are central to the local culture.

confucianism.def: 儒家思想、儒教人物 (單數)
confucianism.ex: He spent years studying ancient Confucianism.

confuse.def: 使混淆、使困惑、搞亂的人的人
confuse.ex: It is easy to confuse these two similar species.

confused.def: 困惑的、混亂的人的人 (或物)
confused.ex: The old woman was in a mentally confused state.

confusing.ex: The instructions were highly confusing for pupils.

confusingly.ex: The sign was stated confusingly for drivers tonight.

confusion.def: 困惑、混亂、騷動的人物 (單數)
confusion.ex: The announcement caused total confusion at station.

confutable.def: 駁得倒的的人的人物 (單數)
confutable.ex: The evidence against him was not easily confutable.

confutation.ex: The scientist gave a full confutation of the theory.

confute.def: 駁斥、證明為誤的人的人
confute.ex: They managed to confute the local argument today.

conga.def: 康加舞、康加鼓的人物 (單數)
conga.col: dance the conga, play conga drums
conga.ex: Everyone started to dance the conga at the party.

congeal.def: 凝結、凝固、凍結的人的人
congeal.ex: The spilled blood began to congeal on the floor.

congenial.def: 意氣相投的、舒適的人的人物
congenial.ex: The shop had a very congenial and warm atmosphere.

congeniality.def: 意氣相投、舒適的人物 (單數)
congeniality.ex: Her social congeniality made her a great local host.

congenital.def: 天生的、先天的人的人物
congenital.ex: The baby was born with a serious congenital defect.

congenitally.ex: She was congenitally blind but led a full life today.

conger.ex: The fisherman caught a massive conger eel in net.

congest.def: 充塞、充血、擁擠的人的人
congest.ex: Construction work began to congest the local roads.

congested.def: 擁擠的、充血的人的人 (或物)
congested.ex: The morning traffic was very heavy and congested.

congestion.ex: He suffered from bad nasal congestion all night.

conglomerate.def: 企業集團、集結、複合的的人的人的人物
conglomerate.ex: The media conglomerate owns ten local newspapers.

conglomeration.def: 集結、混合物的人物 (單數)
conglomeration.ex: The city is a vast conglomeration of small towns.

congratulate.ex: I'd like to congratulate you on your promotion.

congratulation.def: 祝賀、恭喜人物 (複數使用)
congratulation.ex: Please accept our warmest congratulations today.

congratulatory.def: 祝賀的人、慶祝的人的人物
congratulatory.ex: She sent a congratulatory card to the new couple.

congregate.def: 聚集、集合的人的人人物 (單數)
congregate.ex: People began to congregate around the town hall.
